title: Indian Asian Games Badminton Squad Reassembles, Treesa-Gayatri Return
indian_angle: Indian fans will be keen to know the composition of the national badminton squad for the upcoming Asian Games.
summary: The Indian badminton squad for the Asian Games, including the returning Treesa-Gayatri, has reassembled following the Thomas Cup.

title: Tanvi Sharma's Game: More Saina Nehwal Than PV Sindhu?
indian_angle: This article offers an intriguing comparison of young Indian talent Tanvi Sharma's playing style to two of India's greatest badminton icons.
summary: An analysis delves into whether rising Indian shuttler Tanvi Sharma's playing style more closely resembles Saina Nehwal's aggressive approach or PV Sindhu's defensive prowess.

title: Coach Tan Kim Her's Strategy: Pairing Hari-Arjun After Satwik-Chirag Success
indian_angle: Indian badminton enthusiasts will be interested in the strategic decisions behind forming new successful doubles pairs by their national coach.
summary: An in-depth look at how doubles coach Tan Kim Her strategically formed the Indian pair of Hari-Arjun, drawing lessons from the successful partnership of Satwik-Chirag.

title: PV Sindhu Needs More Self-Belief To Overcome An Se-young
indian_angle: This opinion piece critically examines what India's top female shuttler PV Sindhu needs to do to consistently defeat her formidable opponent An Se-young.
summary: An article discusses the mental aspect of PV Sindhu's game, suggesting that increased self-belief is crucial for her to consistently defeat world-class opponent An Se-young.

title: Ayush Shetty Dominates World No. 15 Weng Hong Yang
indian_angle: This highlights a significant victory for young Indian talent Ayush Shetty over a highly-ranked Chinese opponent, showcasing his potential.
summary: Indian shuttler Ayush Shetty delivered a commanding performance, leaving no room for Chinese World No. 15 Weng Hong Yang to gain momentum.
